name: Min Stack
description: Design a stack that supports push, pop, top, and retrieving the minimum element, all in O(1) time. Implement the MinStack class with: __init__() initializes the stack, push(val: int) pushes the element onto the stack, pop() removes the element on top of the stack, top() -> int gets the top element, get_min() -> int retrieves the minimum element in the stack. You may assume that pop, top, and get_min are always called on non-empty stacks.
tests:
def test_basic_operations():
    s = MinStack()
    s.push(3)
    s.push(5)
    assert s.top() == 5
    assert s.get_min() == 3

def test_multiple_mins():
    s = MinStack()
    s.push(2)
    s.push(0)
    s.push(3)
    s.push(0)
    assert s.get_min() == 0
    s.pop()
    assert s.get_min() == 0

def test_pop_updates_min():
    s = MinStack()
    s.push(5)
    s.push(3)
    s.push(1)
    assert s.get_min() == 1
    s.pop()
    assert s.get_min() == 3
    s.pop()
    assert s.get_min() == 5

def test_single_element():
    s = MinStack()
    s.push(42)
    assert s.top() == 42
    assert s.get_min() == 42

def test_negative_values():
    s = MinStack()
    s.push(-2)
    s.push(0)
    s.push(-3)
    assert s.get_min() == -3
    s.pop()
    assert s.get_min() == -2
    assert s.top() == 0

def test_duplicate_values():
    s = MinStack()
    s.push(1)
    s.push(1)
    s.push(1)
    s.pop()
    assert s.get_min() == 1
    assert s.top() == 1

def test_decreasing_then_increasing():
    s = MinStack()
    s.push(3)
    s.push(2)
    s.push(1)
    s.push(4)
    s.push(5)
    assert s.get_min() == 1
    s.pop()
    s.pop()
    s.pop()
    assert s.get_min() == 2
constraints: All operations must run in O(1) time complexity
pop, top, and get_min will always be called on non-empty stacks
